title: EngineArgs -> VllmConfig: Assembling the Stack

commands_run.env: host (CPU, no vllm/CUDA) — companion is pure unit tests, no `import vllm` (grep for 'import vllm' over tests/ + implementation/ returns only comments). Per tester contract, pure unit tests run on host.

commands_run.result: PASS — 保真度检查全部通过 (exit 0, no BLOCKING)

docker.reason: Docker daemon UNAVAILABLE in this environment (`docker info` fails). Not required here: the companion has no `import vllm` and no CUDA — it is pure host unit tests. Real-vLLM behavior cross-check was performed against the materialized blobless source at pin f3fef123 via `git cat-file -p f3fef123:<path>` in instances/vllm/source, which conclusively shows the control flow + return values match the real tree.
docker.vllm_version_intended: ~0.15.1 (container build; behavior cross-checked against pinned source f3fef123 instead)

behavior_cross_check.method: Compared each tested behavior against real vLLM source at pin f3fef123 (git cat-file from instances/vllm/source), not just companion self-consistency.
verified_matches: Executor.get_class backend->class table (mp/uni/external_launcher, type-passthrough, unknown->ValueError) matches vllm/v1/executor/abstract.py:L47-L92
EngineCoreClient.make_client (asyncio&!mp->NotImplementedError; mp&async->make_async_mp_client; mp&!async->SyncMPClient; else->InprocClient) matches vllm/v1/engine/core_client.py:L80-L103
make_async_mp_client DP split (external_lb->DPAsyncMPClient, internal->DPLBAsyncMPClient, single->AsyncMPClient) matches core_client.py:L105-L130
SchedulerConfig.get_scheduler_cls (async->AsyncScheduler else Scheduler; custom passthrough) matches vllm/config/scheduler.py:L168-L188
ParallelConfig backend derivation (single->uni, multi->mp, world>gpus->ValueError) matches vllm/config/parallel.py:L829-L874 (single-node subset)

UniProcExecutor.supports_async_scheduling()==True (uniproc_executor.py:L140-141) and ExecutorWithExternalLauncher(UniProcExecutor) INHERITS True with no override (uniproc_executor.py:L144) — matches real source
base Executor.supports_async_scheduling()==False (abstract.py:L367-372) — used by the truthful raise-path test (NoAsyncExecutor subclass of base Executor)

async_scheduling tri-state (explicit True + unsupported executor -> ValueError; None+pooling -> False; None+supported -> True; explicit False -> False) matches vllm/config/vllm.py:L777-L852 (speculative branches SUBTRACTED since speculative_config is None on this path)

opt-level resolution: enforce_eager override -> NONE/NONE; TORCH_COMPILE_DISABLE=1 -> mode NONE; mode None + opt>O0 -> VLLM_COMPILE else NONE; _set_config_default only fills None fields — matches vllm/config/vllm.py:L904-L976
opt-level presets O0(NONE,flashinfer False) / O1(PIECEWISE,True) / O2(FULL_AND_PIECEWISE,True) / O3==O2 values match vllm/config/vllm.py:L184-L270

prior_blocking_resolved.fix_observed: implementation/config_wiring.py now declares `class ExecutorWithExternalLauncher(UniProcExecutor)` (L936) with NO supports_async_scheduling override, so it inherits True — matching real vLLM uniproc_executor.py:L144. The raise-path test was retargeted to a `NoAsyncExecutor(cw.Executor)` base subclass whose supports_async_scheduling() truthfully returns False (matches abstract.py:L367-372). test_external_launcher_supports_async_scheduling now asserts True and that __post_init__ does NOT raise for external_launcher + async_scheduling=True. Both align with real vLLM at the pin.
prior_blocking_resolved.verified_against_source: instances/vllm/source @ f3fef123: uniproc_executor.py L140-144, abstract.py L367-372

notes: Host pytest (36 passed) and lint_fidelity (no BLOCKING, exit 0) both pass. Every tested behavior was cross-checked against real vLLM source at pin f3fef123 (not just companion self-consistency) and matches. The prior BLOCKING F1 (fabricated external_launcher async support) is resolved by the implementer and re-verified against source. One benign self-consistency note: O3 is implemented as `OPTIMIZATION_LEVEL_03 = OPTIMIZATION_LEVEL_02` (object identity) while real vLLM defines O3 as a separate dict with identical values; observable behavior (cudagraph_mode/flashinfer) is identical and the SOURCE comment documents 'same as O2', so teaching behavior is faithful — not a fidelity defect. Docker unavailable but not required (no import vllm / no CUDA); source cat-file cross-check is sufficient. Gate: APPROVED -> hand to writer.
